arc_1_village_roads.title: Arc 1: Village Roads
arc_1_village_roads.note: Hold the nearest line of road and prove you can keep part of Valerion standing.
arc_1_village_roads.scene_focus: The realm still feels local, practical, and close enough to fail if nobody chooses to act.

arc_2_watchtower_and_shrine.title: Arc 2: Watchtower and Shrine
arc_2_watchtower_and_shrine.note: Push into the older ward-lines where ruined duty and living duty begin to overlap.
arc_2_watchtower_and_shrine.scene_focus: Ruined stones, shrine memory, and border watch now matter as much as simple road safety.

arc_3_stonewatch_burden.title: Arc 3: Stonewatch Burden
arc_3_stonewatch_burden.note: Step into the frontier order proper, where trade, patrol duty, and public trust all become part of the same held line.
arc_3_stonewatch_burden.scene_focus: The village is no longer enough context; Stonewatch reveals how many systems must keep working for the realm to endure.

arc_4_cult_of_ash_rising.title: Arc 4: Cult of Ash Rising
arc_4_cult_of_ash_rising.note: Face the pressure of a faithless enemy trying to unmake what Valerion still preserves through ward and memory.
arc_4_cult_of_ash_rising.scene_focus: Even calm places feel shadowed by a larger threat pressing against every standing shrine and road.

arc_5_frontier_hubs.title: Arc 5: The Frontier Hubs
arc_5_frontier_hubs.note: See how different peoples keep the realm intact in different ways, from coast to wood to hold to ashward cloister.
arc_5_frontier_hubs.scene_focus: Valerion feels larger now: distinct hubs, cultures, and duties all holding separate pieces of the same realm together.

arc_6_vaultreach_watch.title: Arc 6: Vaultreach Watch
arc_6_vaultreach_watch.note: Carry the realm's burden into the ash roads themselves, where only a prepared line can keep old disaster from breaking loose again.
arc_6_vaultreach_watch.scene_focus: The frontier is no longer a border; it is a conscious watch over sealed danger, salvage, and sacred responsibility.
